title: Intro weapon sub-buffer overlap is a print, not a guard (R9/M1.1)

history.note: Landed 25acc40 (fix(bondview): R9 bounds-guard intro weapon load): weapon load bounds-guarded, skips attachment on overlap; GE007_BOND_WEAPON_BUF_FAIL forced-fail hatch added (bondview.c:2920). NOT verified: no registered lane drives the fail path — intro_visual_regression.sh is not ctest-wired and does not exercise the weapon buffer overlap; port_renderer_parity_smoke was green at landing but does not guard this finding. Needs a forced-fail weapon-buf smoke.

Verified: tools/intro_weapon_buf_fail_smoke.sh (ctest port_intro_weapon_buf_fail_smoke) drives BOTH sides of the R9 bounds guard on the Dam intro (level 33, GE007_INTRO_CAMERA_INDEX=5, frame 900). Positive: 0 RENDER-HEALTH, weapon held (right_item_match=356), body present/rendered=356, exit 0. GE007_BOND_WEAPON_BUF_FAIL=1 hatch-forced fail path: EXACTLY 1 [BONDVIEW][RENDER-HEALTH] line, weapon skipped (right_item_match=0), body intact (present/rendered=356 == positive path), no crash -- the T4/M1.1 negative-control numbers. Equal body counts prove the guard skips ONLY the weapon. Fail-on-revert demonstrated: neutralizing skip_weapon makes the fail path load the weapon (right_item_match=356!=0) and reddens the lane. Closes the self-admitted 'lane doesn't guard the fail path' gap (P1c). Full verify: tier-1 ROM-free ctests green, sim_invariance PASS, ENV_FLAGS --check PASS.
